text:
The wise old owl perched high in the oak tree. He could hear the city noises below.

"Hello down there," he said.

A cat meowed.

"The cat talks? Lovely."

Another cat.

"A mouse?!"

"Nocturnal lifestyle."

"What about owls?"

"Just good ears."

The owl chuckled. "You'd think you could talk." He wobbled gently.

"Why?"

"The wind."

"Good thing."

The owl thought a moment.

"You know."

"I do?"

"Some things you'll have to keep to yourself."

"Oh? Yes!"

"Wouldn't tell you."

"You wouldn't?"

"No."

The owl fluffed up his feathers. "What?"

"Just enjoying our time together."

"The owl does that."

"It's soothing."

"For you?"

"I think it's pretty."

The wise old owl laughed.

A bird flew by.

"Say hello to your cousin down there."

The owl tilted his head.

"Well, meet her!"


It was... a mouse.

"No way
